EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A high pressure system over the central Atlantic was driven moderate to fresh winds, resulting choppy seas impacting the local waters of Puerto Rico. The risk of rip currents was high during this period.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers, as well as local newspaper, reported the drowning of a 50-year-old male in La Concha Beach in Condado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Seasonably to above normal temperatures continued for most of Puerto Rico, as well as dew points above 80 degrees. This resulted in heat indices above 112 degrees for many coastal areas of the north, west, and east, as well as valleys in the central interior.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several weather stations reported heat indices above 112 degrees for over two hours. These conditions were observed widespread along the coastal areas and the valleys.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A vigorous tropical crossed the islands and brought squally weather. Frequent showers and thunderstorms impacted region of the eastern half of Puerto Rico, resulting in a few flooding reports.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Managers reported Rio Blanco out of its banks in Road PR-31, interesection with Highway PR-53.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The lack of significant rains across northwestern Puerto Rico resulted in well below normal streams across the area. Additionally, the Guajataca Basin was at observational levels throughout much of the period. This resulted in the area experiencing Severe Drought (D2) conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe Drought conditions were met across northwestern Puerto Rico, due to reservoirs being at observational levels, as well as low streams and dry soils.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As the month progresses, heavy rain did not occur across the extreme northwest of Puerto Rico, where the Guajataca Basin is located. The basin stayed in observational levels. Additionally, rivers were well below normal, soils were dry and the rainfall deficit was increasing as well.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The lack of significant rains created water deficit for the Guajataca Basin, and the basin was at observational levels. Soils were drier than normal due to the increasing rainfall deficit.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 44-year-old male drowned when he was swept away by rip currents in Balneario Cana Gorda in Guanica.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several stations across Puerto Rico reported heat indices above 112 degrees for more than two hours. These conditions were widespread across the coastal area and valleys.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Outer bands from Tropical Storm Philippe, which passed northeast of the islands resulted in heavy rain, river rises, lightning strikes and flooding across various sectors of the islands. Around up to 5 to 10 inches of rain fell over mainly eastern Puerto Rico from Oct 1st through October 5th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Managers indicated that the power station at Culebra was struck by lightning, causing a fire and thus, a power outage across the island. Electrical workers restored service before sunrise on Tuesday.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow-moving showers and isolated thunderstorms developed late in the afternoon on the 11th of January. This activity lasted through the evening hours, and rainfall amounts of up to 5 inches were observed in Bayamon. Additional showers affected nearby municipalities and produced flooding across the central and eastern interior sections of Puerto Rico, including the San Juan metropolitan area. However, the most significant impacts were noted across Bayamon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 call center reported of a flooded street with one person trapped in a car by PR-167 and Comerio street, close to the Bayamon Christian Academy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northwesterly swell and choppy wind-driven seas produced large breaking waves and rough surf conditions along the northern coast of Puerto Rico during mid-January.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man drowned on Jobos Beach in Isabela around 3:30 pm after being swept away by rip currents. Another man who was injured in the incident was transported to a hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of wind-driven seas and a small long-period northesterly swell caused rough surf conditions and life-threatening rip currents across the Atlantic beaches of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 35-year-old man was swept away by rip currents at Vacia Talega beach and drowned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers increased in frequency across the eastern third of Puerto Rico due to the interaction of a surface trough and a TUTT. This activity resulted in a mudslide in Morovis.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported a mudslide near Road PR-159, near JMV Carwash. The mudslide was affecting the entrance to near 14 houses, but no other impacts was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The islands were in between two areas of enhanced moisture. To the west, a cold front was approaching, and from the Leeward Islands, another patch of moisture was approaching. The available moisture combined with local effects to generate strong showers and thunderstorms that generated local flooding and mudslides.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported water reaching houses in Villa Clarita.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Although relatively tranquil seas were observed across the local waters on May 27th, the sea breeze along the north-central coastal areas of Puerto Rico promoted a moderate risk of rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers, as well as reports from the news, indicated that a 52-year-old male drowned at Punta Salinas Beach in Toa Baja.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a surface low and deep-layer trough northwest of Puerto Rico created a band of convergence across the islands. This produced numerous showers and thunderstorms across the eastern sections of Puerto Rico on May 22, 2023, which resulted in river flooding in Naguabo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management officials reported that Rio Blanco rose out of its banks due to excessive rainfall and flooded an area of roadway PR-31 around km 8.9 in Naguabo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Higher than normal moisture content and east to southeasterly winds, combined with local and diurnal effects, led to the development of thunderstorms over the San Juan metro area on May 19th.  Between 1 and 3 inches of rain were observed with this activity across Carolina and San Juan, which caused urban flooding between Isla Verde and Condado areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 call center reported flood waters affecting a parking lot and portions of an apartment complex on Ave. Isla Verde next to the Pueblo supermarket.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Higher than normal moisture content and east to southeasterly winds, combined with local and diurnal effects, led to the development of thunderstorms over the San Juan metro area on May 19th. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mar del Norte Street in Carolina was flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally induced afternoon thunderstorms on May 17th produced up to 3 inches of rain over portions of the southwest quadrant of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 call center reported flood waters affecting a house near Road 117 between streets 7 and 9 in Lajas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper-level low promoted a favorable environment for showers and thunderstorms to develop in the afternoon. This produced heavy rainfall over the San Juan metro area, where flooding was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 911 call center report indicated flooding affecting a residence on Lafayette Street in San Juan.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a small northerly swell and a high pressure system driving fresh to strong winds across the islands resulted in choppy and confused seas across the Atlantic waters. As a result, breaking waves of 7 to 9 feet resulted in a high rip current risks for the north-facing beaches of the islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 70-year-old male was swept away by rip currents at Flamenco Beach in Culebra.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unsettled weather with numerous showers and thunderstorms were observed due to an upper level cut-off low strengthening northwest of Puerto Rico. Strong divergence aloft resulted in hail, and the heavy rain left flooding, mudslides and rivers overflowing.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two individuals were trapped inside a car due to a flood on PR-3, near Calle Ashford.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching frontal boundary, along with patchy moisture reaching the area, and increased instability due to a developing low north of the islands, resulted in the formation of areas of showers and thunderstorms. These storms resulted in several reports of flooding, mudslides, and even a funnel cloud.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Manager reported flooding along Road PR-1, Sector La Barra. The road was closed due to the flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching frontal boundary, along with patchy moisture reaching the area, and increased instability due to a developing low north of the islands, resulted in the formation of areas of showers and thunderstorms. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak trough near the Leeward islands was maintaining a weak steering flow over the local islands. Instability aloft was increasing as mid level temperatures cool down. The combination of these factors, along with strong diurnal heating and enough moisture in the lower levels resulted in very strong showers and isolated thunderstorms over the area. Several reports of mudslides and flooding were received.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported a tree down at road PR-815 in Naranjito. Wind speeds were around 25 mph.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: An individual was trapped in a car located on a bridge over the river at road PR-647.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak trough near the Leeward islands was maintaining a weak steering flow over the local islands. Instability aloft was increasing as mid level temperatures cool down. The combination of these factors, along with strong diurnal heating and enough moisture in the lower levels resulted in very strong showers and isolated thunderstorms over the area. Several reports of mudslides and flooding were received.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported flooding at Calle Georgetti, which is the road opposite to the Municipal Police Station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northeasterly swell combined with locally generated wind waves produced confused choppy seas and large breaking waves during the first week of March.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 49-year-old woman was swept away by rip currents while surfing at Steps beach in Rincon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal passage produced a weak landspout over the eastern interior of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On March 16, a landspout developed over highway 30 and moved south-southeast at around 20 mph. On its path, the landspout affected the April Garden strip mall with winds of around 65 mph affecting local business signs, awnings, and a free-standing street light pole that fell due to the winds. Small tree branches were also affected by the winds. There were about 325 meters of land spout-to-ground interaction based on damages and witness reports.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northerly swell and wind-driven seas resulted in large breaking waves along the north coast of Puerto Rico during the week of March 16th. Life-threatening rip currents and high surf conditions were observed with this event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 911 operator received a report of a drowning at La Pared beach in Luquillo. Police officers found a male between 25 and 35 years old drowned on the shore.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northeasterly swell combined with locally generated wind waves produced confused choppy seas and large breaking waves during the first week of March.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A group of five tourists was swept away by rip currents at Caracoles beach in Arecibo. Three were able to swim back to the coastline. However, a father and a son drowned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northerly swell and wind-driven seas resulted in large breaking waves along the north coast of Puerto Rico during the week of March 16th. Life-threatening rip currents and high surf conditions were observed with this event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two men were swept by rip currents while trying to rescue a father and his son who were asking for help because they could not return to shore.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate to locally fresh trades and small northerly swell created choppy seas up to 6 feet and occasionally higher across the Atlantic waters. This resulted in life-threatening rip currents along the northern coast of Puerto Rico during the weekend of March 24-26.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tourist male, approximately 40 years old, was swept away by rip currents and drowned at Las Picuas beach in Rio Grande.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate to locally fresh trades and small northerly swell created choppy seas up to 6 feet and occasionally higher across the Atlantic waters. This resulted in life-threatening rip currents along the northern coast of Puerto Rico during the weekend of March 24-26.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 55-year-old male tourist was swept away by rip currents at Escondida beach in Fajardo and drowned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Higher than normal moisture content combined with a southeasterly wind flow produced showers and thunderstorms from the streamer that developed from the El Yunque area into the San Juan metro area. The Doppler radar estimated between 2 and 5 inches of rain in and around Carolina, Hato Rey, and San Juan, where flooding was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported on Simon Madera Avenue, near the intersection of the Ramal 8. A house and the street were affected by the flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Higher than normal moisture content combined with a southeasterly wind flow produced showers and thunderstorms from the streamer that developed from the El Yunque area into the San Juan metro area. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported in Calle M in Hato Rey.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Higher than normal moisture content combined with a southeasterly wind flow produced showers and thunderstorms from the streamer that developed from the El Yunque area into the San Juan metro area. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire burned three houses on the 31st of March in the municipality of Moca.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Around 2:00 PM AST, firefighters and other agencies reported six houses being affected by a wildfire in Barrio Maria in Moca and Aguada. The fire covered 120 acres. The fire also affected a nearby junker, and several vehicles were burned down. The fire department reported that no people were injured, but over 15 people were evacuated from the zone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm developed over northwestern Puerto Rico on July 16th and produced a tornado near Road 411 on the border of Barrio Asomante and  Barrio Guayabo around 3:08 PM AST and lasted around 11 minutes. The tornado was moving towards the north-northwest from Barrio Guayabo to Balneario Pico de Piedra, where it transitioned into a waterspout over the coastal waters before dissipating.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management reported that a preliminary short-duration tornado damaged power poles and cables and caused them to fall over a one-way street near Epifanio Estrada School in Aguada. This was reported in Road PR-411, Km 2.9. Part of this road and the one-way street were obstructed by the debris. One house suffered major damage, including partial loss of the roof. This resulted in significant interior damage to the house. Another house lost its roof covering material and metal siding. Numerous trees were uprooted and snapped along the tornado track.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The lacks of wetting rains caused streamflow to run below to much below normal for northwestern Puerto Rico. Additionally, the Guajataca basin received less rain than usual, resulting in the reservoir to reach observational levels.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe Drought were introduced for northwestern Puerto Rico due to the lacks of rain, causing rivers and reservoirs to go below to much below normal in the area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe Drought were introduced for northwestern Puerto Rico due to the lacks of rain, causing rivers and reservoirs to go below to much below normal in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Extremely hot conditions persisted through the entire month of June for all the climatological sites. Several factors contributed to this hot spell. First of all, the wind flow was persistently from the south to southeast bringing typically warm air into the area, and once that air mass crossed the Cordillera Central In Puerto Rico, the air warmed even more when descending. Second, subsidence associated with a near stationary mid-upper level ridge warmed the air mass. The extremely anomalous sea surface temperature possibly increased the moisture content in the environment, causing very high heat indices. The lack of clouds and rain also allowed for more sunshine to reach the surface, so temperatures rose quickly each day. Finally, urban heating exacerbated the very high temperatures in the region. Under these conditions, twenty (20) excessive heat warnings and twenty-seven (27)  heat advisories were issued for several parts of Puerto Rico. Several stations across Puerto Rico, including Culebra reported heat indices surpassing 112 degrees, and on the hottest days, up to 120 to 122 degrees. The areas with the highest temperatures were the coastal and urban areas of the north and west, the valleys of the eastern interior, and Culebra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several Mesonet stations reported heat indices surpassing 108 degrees (local heat advisory criteria), and even 112 degrees (excessive heat warning criteria) for a prolonged period of days, mainly from 10 AM to 4 PM AST. The reports were widespread along the station network.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Managers indicated that Rio Maunabo went out of its banks, overflowing|over a bridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave with its axis between Puerto Rico and the Mona Passage produced strong thunderstorms with gusty winds and flooding rains across Puerto Rico on the 17th of August. In addition, a waterspout developed off the northwestern coast of PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 911 Call Center reported flooding due to heavy rain at Calle C intersection with Calle D in Barrio Mameyal, Dorado PR.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave with its axis between Puerto Rico and the Mona Passage produced strong thunderstorms with gusty winds and flooding rains across Puerto Rico on the 17th of August. In addition, a waterspout developed off the northwestern coast of PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media reports indicated strong winds at Urb. Hermanas Davila in Bayamon. Winds caused damage to small plants and a backyard roof.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media reports indicated strong winds due to thunderstorms in Barrio Maria, Sector |Ana L. P��rez interior, PR-110 km 9.1. Some of the damage includes plantain trees and |other smaller plants.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: External bands from Tropical Storm Franklin produced showers and thunderstorms, along with gusty winds and flooding rains across portions of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers indicated that road PR-31 in Naguabo is impassable due to flood waters from Rio Blanco.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: External bands from Tropical Storm Franklin produced showers and thunderstorms, along with gusty winds and flooding rains across portions of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 911 Call Center reported a landslide in PR-9918 in Yabucoa.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: External bands from Tropical Storm Franklin produced showers and thunderstorms, along with gusty winds and flooding rains across portions of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 911 Call Center reported a downed tree next to Office Gallery on Road 172 in Caguas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Diurnal heating aided in the development of showers along the interior and western portions of Puerto Rico. The islands were over an area of localized enhanced moisture, but there was no dynamics at the mid and upper levels.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported at Calle Malasia, intersection with Javillo and Jose Gonzalez streets. The road was closed by emergency managers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The local winds and a fading northeasterly swell where creating breaking waves above 6 feet along the coastal areas of Puerto Rico and Culebra. A High Rip Current Statement was in effect for these beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 42-year old male drowned after being swept away by rip currents at Playa Flamenco in Culebra. Another person was rescued when was also swept away by these rip currents.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Marine conditions were deteriorating as a northerly swell arrived to the Atlantic waters of Puerto Rico. As a result, the risk of rip currents increased to high, and a High Rip Current Statement was in effect.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Managers reported that a 13 year old male was swept away by rip currents in Montones Beach in Isabela. After about a week of searching, authorities stated that he is presumed dead and the body was carried out to sea.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture pulled into the region by TC Tammy (by that time well north of the area), in combination with an upper-level trough, a frontal boundary and a pre-frontal trough, produced numerous showers and thunderstorms across the islands. Between 3 and 7 inches of rain were observed across the northern, eastern, and central portions of Puerto Rico. Several Debris Flow episodes and at least 1 case of wind damage were reported. Swells from Tammy also produced hazardous marine and coastal conditions, resulting in coastal flood in several northern areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Vehicle was trapped with people inside due to a flooded road in San Patricio Mall near a Walgreens and a Triple-s Building.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: External feeding bands and moisture field from hurricane Tammy moving well east and north of the region reached the local islands. Additionally, a nearby TUTT and remnants of T.C. Sean also helped promote rain activity. Flooding reports where received as strong showers and thunderstorms affected the island leaving around from 2 to 5 inches of rain, particularly across Eastern Interior, E/SE, Western-Western Interior and South-Central Puerto Rico as well as the metro area. A funnel cloud was detected within this activity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four cars had people trapped in them due to ponding water on Avenida Las Americas in Ponce near a Tasty Chicken restaurant. The emergency manager reported that the flooding was above the tires of the trucks and his waist. Ave. Las Americas was closed as it was impassable from Tasty Chicken to La Secretaria de Recreacion y Deportes Building. The authorities have the Avenida closed. The people trapped were rescued.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A vehicle with two people trapped was located in a flooded Virgilio Biaggi Rd. between Urb. Perla del Sur and Urb. Villa Grillasca. This road is adjacent of the flooded Las Americas Ave. Authorities where rescuing them at that moment. Radar estimated 1 to 4 inches of rain in the Ponce municipality around 6:30 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Outer bands from Tropical Storm Philippe, which passed northeast of the islands resulted in heavy rain, river rises, lightning strikes and flooding across various sectors of the islands. Around up to 5 to 10 inches of rain fell over mainly eastern Puerto Rico from Oct 1st through October 5th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management reported flooding at PR-31 due to Rio Blanco out of its banks. This road is closed and impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Accumulated rainfall of up to around 3 to 4 inches over the San Sebastian Municipality in northwestern Puerto Rico served to saturate soils over the course of October 1st through 7th. Afternoon convection over northwestern Puerto Rico on October 9th where only up to 0.25 but served to trigger a mudslide.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Management reported a mudslide at PR-435 in Parcelas San Gabriel.

This produced showers and thunderstorms over the eastern and northern sections of Puerto Rico, including the San Juan Metro area. Urban and flash flooding was observed across these areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 976 was impassable near Bo. Sector Naranjo.

This produced showers and thunderstorms over the eastern and northern sections of Puerto Rico, including the San Juan Metro area. Urban and flash flooding was observed across these areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Toa Baja EM reported a car trapped in flood waters along road PR-865 in Villa Clemente, near the American Gas Station. EM and Fire Department tow truck removed the car from the area. No one was injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As hurricane Lee moved well north of the region, external feeding bands from the hurricane reached the local islands. Additionally, the moisture advected into the region, contributed to the development of strong showers along the Cordillera Central and southern Puerto Rico. A few flooding reports, and even a funnel cloud was detected within this activity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several reports from the general public indicated a funnel cloud, observed from Salinas Speedway toward the mountains.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A TV Meteorologist received several pictures of flooding along the roads of Urb. Vistas de Naguabo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Although the influence in the local weather due to Hurricane Lee was lessening, the wind flow remained from the south or slightly southeasterly. Under this wind flow, showers moved the Caribbean Sea into southern Puerto Rico, resulting in some water surges and rivers reaching flood stage. Strong thunderstorms also developed along the northern municipalities of Puerto Rico, with gusty winds causing minor damage to zinc roof and tree limbs down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A media broadcaster reported that Rio Grande de Patillas was out of its banks along road PR-184, near Barrio Jagual and at the other side of El Escondite de Don Claudio. The road remained passable.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A media broadcaster reported that PR-3 was flooded in front of Salinas Speedway. The road, however, remained passable.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A social media post showed photos of broken tree limbs from gusty winds of a strong thunderstorm. These limbs were obstructing part of PR-2 near Urb. Colinas and Sector Alto el Cabro. As a result, extensive traffic queues as emergency managers worked to clear the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The tropical cyclones centered well north of the islands, into the open Atlantic waters, continued to generate swells that deteriorated marine and coastal conditions along the northern coast of Puerto Rico. At least two lives were lost due to strong rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers and broadcast media reported a 31-year-old female that was found drowned near La Pocita in Loiza. The victim was likely swept away by rip currents.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A northerly s well from Hurricane Lee deteriorated marine and coastal conditions along the area. Breaking waves just shy from 14 feet were observed along many areas in northern Puerto Rico. The breaking waves managed to bring sand into vulnerable roads.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers indicated that braking waves were reaching PR-187 at Playa Vacia  Talega. The road was passable, but sand was accumulating in the lanes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A northerly s well from Hurricane Lee deteriorated marine and coastal conditions along the area. Breaking waves just shy from 14 feet were observed along many areas in northern Puerto Rico. The breaking waves managed to bring sand into vulnerable roads.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 66-year-old male drowned in Poza del Obispo, near road PR-681 in Arecibo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The tropical cyclones centered well north of the islands, into the open Atlantic waters, continued to generate swells that deteriorated marine and coastal conditions along the northern coast of Puerto Rico. At least two lives were lost due to strong rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 15-year-old male was swept away by rip currents at a beach near Los Kioskos de Luquillo. The victim was recovered on 09/18/2023 around 1:15 PM AST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough crossed the islands, resulting in an increase in shower and thunderstorm activity, when combined with local effects. Some localized flooding was reported with this event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported flooding along road PR-723 at Sector La Pulguilla in Barrio Betania (near Academia Menonita Betania), due to excessive runoff from previous rainfall. These runoff managed to reach a tributary of Rio Usabon between Coamo and Aibonito, resulting in several roads affected.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A humid air mass was filtering into the local islands from the east. This area of inclement weather resulted in an increase in frequency of showers and thunderstorms. Under an unstable atmosphere, additional convective development was observed in the south of Puerto Rico in the afternoon hours. One of these storms generated a funnel cloud.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report from the public and broadcast media indicated that a funnel cloud was observed from PFC Santos Cruz Aviles in Salinas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Excessive heat conditions have not stopped in the month of September. Heat indices reached 108F or more almost each day. These observations are widespread along the Mesonet, covering most of the urban and coastal areas. The only zones that did not experience heat indices above 108F are the central interior and western interior due to elevation.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several stations in the mesonet reported heat indices values of at least 108F, surpassing 112F some days, for over two hours. These conditions met the local criteria for heat advisories and excessive heat warnings.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hazardous coastal conditions where observed across the northern coastline from Rincon to Fajardo due to long-dominant period northwesterly to northerly swell and vigorous to fresh winds impacting the regional waters. High Surf Advisories and a High Risk of Rip Currents were in effect for Fajardo, where four people were swept away by strong rip currents at Playa Escondida (one fatality and three injured).
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On December 9th, Emergency Managers in Fajardo, PR, reported that four people were swept away by strong rip currents at Playa Escondida, Fajardo. Emergency Managers successfully rescued three out of the four individuals, but unfortunately, a young 25-year-old adult from San Lorenzo, Puerto Rico, drowned. The body was found about 1,500 ft from the coastline on December 11th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Life-threatening rip currents due to rough surf and large breaking waves were affecting northern and eastern Puerto Rico, Culebra and the northern USVI. Nearshore buoys indicated breaking waves between 9 to 11 feet during the morning of Dec 13th. Later that that a woman was presumed to be a victim of breaking waves at the Barceloneta coast near Isla Roque Beach. A High Surf Advisory is in effect for all surf zones across northern and eastern Puerto Rico, Culebra, and northern USVI.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In the morning hours of today, the drowned body of a 63-year-old woman, who had been missing since Dec 13th, was found. In the police investigation, it is presumed that she was a victim of large breaking waves while she was walking along the rocky area of the Barceloneta coast near Isla Roque Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Wind Advisory was in effect for PR and the USVI from Tuesday afternoon to Thursday morning. This was due to the interaction of a surface high-pressure north of the area and a strong low-pressure system northeast of the Caribbean resulting in windy conditions over the local area. Sustained winds at 20 to 30 mph with frequent gusts around 35 to 45 mph were forecast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Based on a local weather broadcasters social media page, we received a report on a fallen tree due to windy trades at Pajonal Neighborhood in the Florida municipality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wet pattern peaked Friday, December 22nd, and Saturday, December 23rd, as a front north of Puerto Rico and the USVI, sank southwards over the northeast Caribbean to end the week. Under this pattern, fragments of moisture brought periods of showery weather. A mid- to upper-level trough and associated jet maxima provided favorable conditions for shower development. Periods of locally heavy rains caused ponding of water on roads, flooding and hazardous driving conditions. A landslide was also reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Landslide reported by a 911 employee in Road 690, in Vega Alta, near Vega Baja.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon convective activity over south-central to southwest PR produced a funnel cloud over SW Puerto Rico before gradually dissipating and moving offshore on December 7th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several public reports on social media mentioned a funnel cloud over Cabo Rojo, PR. No reports of it touching down. No reports of damage.
